prompt: Ping me if our payment API error rate climbs above 5%.
note: the skill examines the code first and proposes a tracking plan, then WAITS for approval before the create; the eval grants that approval up-front (run.ts standing-approval clause). Full chain: an alert fires on a metric, a metric rolls up an event. List events, metrics AND alerts first to check none already exist, then create what's missing. expect_state confirms the metric + alert landed. Uses a 'payment' domain (not 'checkout') so the metric it creates never collides with the experiments case's checkout metric.

prompt: Mute the signup-conversion alert for now.
note: referenced by name — list to resolve its id, then pause (update enabled:false). setup pre-seeds the signup-conversion alert so this mutate case is self-contained (an alert can't be created by the framework's flag/event seed alone — see setupState).

prompt: Bump the checkout error-rate alert to fire at 10% instead.
note: referenced by name — list to resolve its id, then update the threshold (not a new rule). setup pre-seeds the checkout error-rate alert so this no longer depends on the error-rate-threshold case having created one first.

prompt: What alerts do we have set up?
note: read-only listing must not touch any rule
